TFG quick course

Premium service in 6 minutes

3 short lessons

Big idea

Sell with clarity
not pressure.

A bold, short-format learning page inspired by TFG’s clean editorial brand language, but adapted for fast retail learning with bigger type, simpler interactions and more energy.

Outcome

Know what to say

Format

One-page microcourse

Style

Editorial + premium retail

Progress

1

of 3 sections

Modern learning should look closer to a campaign page than a legacy LMS screen.

Keep modules short, strong and visual. Use one key message, one clear action and one memorable recap.

What learners get

Three big outcomes.

These cards are deliberately oversized and sparse so the page feels premium and fast.

01

Spot the moment

Notice the customer cue quickly and move before the moment is lost.

02

Lead with confidence

Use a short, clear line that feels premium, natural and on-brand.

03

Close the loop

Turn good service into the next action: fit, add-on, loyalty or follow-up.

Visual direction

Large text.
Light structure.
Strong image blocks.

Short pages with oversized type

One strong idea per section

Large editorial imagery, tightly cropped

Cards that feel like campaign tiles, not LMS widgets

Recommended section pattern

One key idea.
One interaction.
One recap.

This pattern keeps fast courses consistent. It also makes AI-assisted course production much cleaner, because each block can map neatly to a component and a small set of props.

Section recipe

01 Hero statement
02 Key teaching point
03 Scenario choice
04 One action to try

Practice block

Match the pitch to the moment.

Tap or hover cards

Quick recap

01

Lead with a useful point, not a generic compliment.

02

Keep the language short enough to feel natural on the floor.

03

Move from explanation to action: style, fit, add-on, or next step.

Last block

TFG learning should feel premium, fast and unmistakably retail.

Use this starter as the base layout, swap the placeholders for approved TFG imagery, and then build reusable components for hero, outcomes, scenario cards and recap sections.

Start course View recap
